stem: A finite group \( G \) has order \( [[g]] \), and \( N \trianglelefteq G \) is a normal subgroup with \( |N| = [[n]] \). Find the order of the quotient group \( G/N \).
solution: The elements of \( G/N \) are the cosets of \( N \), and there are \( |G|/|N| = [[g]]/[[n]] = [[answer]] \) of them, so \( |G/N| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( |G| \) itself, forgetting to quotient by \( N \) at all.
This is \( |N| \), the wrong quantity for the quotient's order.
This subtracts \( |N| \) from \( |G| \) instead of dividing.
This adds \( |N| \) to \( |G| \) instead of dividing.
This is off by one above the correct quotient order.
This is off by one below the correct quotient order.

stem: Let \( G = \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) (integers mod \([[n]]\) under addition) and let \( N = \langle [[a]] \rangle \) be the cyclic subgroup generated by \([[a]]\). Since \(G\) is abelian, \(N\) is automatically normal. Find the order of the quotient group \( G/N \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( |N| \) itself, the wrong side of the quotient computation.
This is \( |G| \), forgetting to account for \(N\) at all.
This mistakes the generator \( [[a]] \) itself for the quotient order.
This subtracts the quotient order from \(n\) instead of computing \(\gcd\) correctly.
This is off by one above the correct quotient order.
This is off by one below the correct quotient order.

stem: Let \( G = S_{[[n]]} \), with \( N = A_{[[n]]} \) the alternating group of even permutations. \(N\) is normal in \(G\) (it is the kernel of the sign homomorphism), with \( |G| = [[n]]! \) and \( |N| = [[n]]!/2 \). Find the order of the quotient group \( G/N \).
solution: \( |G/N| = |G|/|N| = [[n]]!/([[n]]!/2) = 2 \), so \(G/N\) is a group of order \(2\) (isomorphic to \( \mathbb{Z}_2 \)).
distractor_rationales: This is \( |G| \) itself, forgetting to quotient by \(N\) at all.
This is \( |N| \), the wrong side of the quotient computation.
This mistakes \(n\) itself for the quotient order.
This mistakes \(n-1\) for the quotient order.
This overstates the quotient order.
This is the trivial group's order, forgetting \(N\) is a PROPER subgroup here.

stem: Let \( G = D_{[[n]]} \) be the dihedral group of order \(2\cdot[[n]]\) (symmetries of a regular \([[n]]\)-gon), and let \( N \) be the subgroup of rotations by multiples of \([[d]]\) steps, so \( |N| = [[n]]/[[d]] = [[h]] \). \(N\) is normal in \(G\) (rotations are inverted, not mixed with reflections, under conjugation). Find the order of the quotient group \( G/N \).
solution: \( |G/N| = |G|/|N| = 2\cdot[[n]]/[[h]] = [[answer]] \), so \(G/N\) is (isomorphic to) a dihedral group of order \([[answer]]\).
distractor_rationales: This is the rotation step \([[d]]\), not the order of the quotient group.
This is \( |G| \) itself, forgetting to quotient by \(N\).
This is \( |N| \), the wrong side of the quotient computation.
This inverts the roles of \(n\) and \(h\) in the division.
This is off by one above the correct quotient order.
This is off by one below the correct quotient order.

stem: Let \( G = \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \times \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), so \( |G| = [[m]]\cdot[[n]] \), and let \( N = \mathbb{Z}_{[[m]]} \times \{0\} \), a normal subgroup (since \(G\) is abelian) of order \([[m]]\). Find the order of the quotient group \( G/N \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( |N| \), the order of the subgroup, not the quotient.
This is \( |G| \) itself, forgetting to quotient by \(N\).
This subtracts the two factor orders instead of using the quotient.
This adds the two factor orders instead of using the quotient.
This is off by one above the correct quotient order.
This is off by one below the correct quotient order.
